_meta.purpose: Single source-of-truth mapping effect/zone/palette names to binary IDs for the Thomas helmet light show system. Includes machine-readable definitions used by the scene compiler AND human-readable annotations for AI agents authoring scenes. The compiler reads `id`, `type`, `range`, and `zones` fields. AI agents read everything including `description`, `when_to_use`, `when_not_to_use`, `notes`, and `examples`.
companion_docs: sd_card_animation_architecture.md — binary bundle format and ESP-NOW protocol

_authoring_guidance_for_ai_agents.overview: When asked to draft a scene for a Daft Punk song, an AI agent should: (1) consult the song's structure (intro, verse, chorus, bridge, outro) and beat grid; (2) for each segment, pick a palette that matches the section's energy and aesthetic; (3) within each segment, place cues that translate the music's character to light — beat-driven effects on rhythmic sections, breathing/sustained effects on atmospheric sections, sprites/text/VU on the matrix during high-information moments; (4) prefer fewer cues with longer durations (let effects play out) over many short cues; (5) mirror by default — use `BOTH_EARS` and `BOTH_VISORS` instead of explicit left/right unless asymmetry is intentional.
common_authoring_mistakes: Authoring per-beat cues when a single beat-aware effect would do the same thing more cleanly. Use EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT for a whole chorus, not 64 separate brightness cues.
Forgetting that matrix brightness is capped at 45/255 in firmware — authoring matrix params at 200 just wastes parameter space (engine will clamp).
Ignoring the connector LED defaults — they already provide a baseline gold beat flash + rainbow ring. Override only when needed.
Mixing palettes mid-segment for no clear musical reason. Pick a palette per segment, stick with it, change at segment boundaries.
Using EFFECT_FULL_FLASH too often. It's a punctuation effect, not a base layer.

PAL_DISCOVERY.description: The workhorse palette. Helmet gold, Stella pink (magenta), Octave cyan, and a deep blue base. Inspired by Discovery-era Daft Punk visuals and the Crescendolls helmet sketch.
PAL_DISCOVERY.when_to_use: Default choice for most Discovery-era tracks (Crescendolls, Aerodynamic groove sections, Digital Love, Harder Better Faster Stronger). Energetic but not aggressive.
PAL_DISCOVERY.when_not_to_use: Quiet ballad-like passages (use PAL_OCEAN or PAL_INTRO_WARM). Aggressive breakdowns (use PAL_RED_BREAKDOWN). Cool nighttime atmospheric (use PAL_OCEAN).
PAL_DISCOVERY.notes: Tested on real WS2812 LEDs in the Crescendolls helmet sketch. These RGB values are known to look right on hardware.

PAL_FIRE.description: Black → red → orange → yellow → white. Classic fire ramp.
PAL_FIRE.when_to_use: Energy peaks, climactic moments, sections that escalate (e.g. a building chorus, a climax). Works well as a brightness ramp — gradient_index can represent intensity.
PAL_FIRE.when_not_to_use: Most Discovery-era tracks (too aggressive). Calm passages. Mirroring scenes that need cool tones.
PAL_FIRE.notes: Untested on hardware. May need yellow/white tuning after first real-LED testing.

PAL_OCEAN.description: Black → deep blue → cyan → light blue → white. Cool, atmospheric.
PAL_OCEAN.when_to_use: Atmospheric passages, calm bridges, outros that fade to peace. Pairs well with EFFECT_AMBIENT_BREATHE.
PAL_OCEAN.when_not_to_use: High-energy beat-driven sections. Anything that wants warmth.
PAL_OCEAN.notes: Untested on hardware. Blue channel is heavily attenuated by white-point correction (0.65×), so RGB values here should look like the corrected output.

PAL_FOREST.description: Black → dark green → green → yellow-green → cream. Earthy, organic.
PAL_FOREST.when_to_use: Reserved palette slot. No strong Discovery-era use case yet. Plausibly useful for cover-y/folkier tracks or for visual variety.
PAL_FOREST.when_not_to_use: Most Daft Punk material — they don't really do 'earthy.'
PAL_FOREST.notes: Untested on hardware. May be removed in future versions if no clear use emerges.

PAL_NEON.description: Hot pink, cyan, lime, yellow, purple. High-saturation pop colors with no neutrals.
PAL_NEON.when_to_use: Energetic, playful sections. Crescendolls' more whimsical bars. Faster, brighter Daft Punk material.
PAL_NEON.when_not_to_use: Atmospheric or moody sections. Anything that wants a sense of darkness or depth (no black/dark in the gradient).

PAL_GOLD_WARM.description: Helmet gold variations. Dim gold → mid gold → bright gold → amber → cream. No cool tones.
PAL_GOLD_WARM.when_to_use: Thomas-themed moments (his helmet IS gold). Solo gold-focused sections without competing colors. Vintage / classic Daft Punk visual quotes.
PAL_GOLD_WARM.when_not_to_use: When you need contrast — this palette is all warm tones, no opposites.
PAL_GOLD_WARM.notes: Helmet gold RGB (255, 184, 0) is sourced from the Crescendolls helmet sketch — tested on real LEDs.

PAL_COOL_SWEEP.description: Cool-to-warm sweep used in Aerodynamic groove sections. Deep blue → cyan → warm gold.
PAL_COOL_SWEEP.when_to_use: Aerodynamic-style groove sections — the song that explicitly uses this palette in the original sketch. Sections where you want gradual warming or cooling within a single cue.
PAL_COOL_SWEEP.when_not_to_use: Beat-driven sections where the palette needs hard transitions (use PAL_DISCOVERY instead).
PAL_COOL_SWEEP.notes: Closely matches CRGB(0, 24, 96) and CRGB(255, 192, 32) from the Aerodynamic groove renderer.

PAL_RED_BREAKDOWN.description: Aggressive red. Black gaps for tension. Used in Aerodynamic's break section.
PAL_RED_BREAKDOWN.when_to_use: Breakdown sections, ominous moments, tension building. Aerodynamic's break (60.7–89.6s in the original sketch).
PAL_RED_BREAKDOWN.when_not_to_use: Most of any song — this is a punctuation palette. Sustained use becomes overwhelming.
PAL_RED_BREAKDOWN.notes: Includes black at index 0 so gradient-based effects can create gaps/dropouts. Tested on hardware via the original Aerodynamic break sketch.

PAL_INTRO_WARM.description: Warm amber/orange for intros. Slow burn, calm energy.
PAL_INTRO_WARM.when_to_use: Slow openings, song intros, sections that build from quiet to energetic. Aerodynamic's intro (0–13.6s in the original sketch). Pairs naturally with EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE.
PAL_INTRO_WARM.when_not_to_use: Once a song has 'kicked in' — intro_warm is a setup color, not a payoff color.
PAL_INTRO_WARM.notes: RGB (192, 64, 8) is sourced from the original Aerodynamic intro sketch — tested on real LEDs.

PAL_MONO_WHITE.description: Pure grayscale. Black → gray → white. No hue.
PAL_MONO_WHITE.when_to_use: When you want pure brightness modulation without color (effect of a strobe or breathing without hue). Stark visual contrast moments.
PAL_MONO_WHITE.when_not_to_use: Almost any sustained section — too clinical. White hits hard on the eyes at full brightness.

PAL_MAGENTA_CYAN.description: Just magenta and cyan with hard transitions. Crescendolls' beat-cycle colors.
PAL_MAGENTA_CYAN.when_to_use: When a scene needs hard binary color transitions tied to beats — magenta on beat 1, cyan on beat 3 patterns (Crescendolls Segment A2 in the original sketch).
PAL_MAGENTA_CYAN.when_not_to_use: When smooth color blending is wanted. The gradient_stops here are intentionally flat-then-jump.
PAL_MAGENTA_CYAN.notes: Magenta and cyan are the exact Stella/Octave colors from the Crescendolls helmet. Hardware-tested.

PAL_RESERVED_11.description: Reserved. Likely future PAL_HUMAN_AFTER_ALL (stark black/red/white). Not yet defined.
PAL_RESERVED_11.when_to_use: Do not use in scenes — referencing this palette will fail validation.

PAL_RESERVED_12.description: Reserved. Likely future PAL_RAM (sunset oranges + electric blue). Not yet defined.
PAL_RESERVED_12.when_to_use: Do not use in scenes — referencing this palette will fail validation.

LEFT_EAR.description: Left ear diamond board, 16 LEDs in an EKG-like profile.
LEFT_EAR.when_to_use_directly: When intentionally creating asymmetric scenes where the two ears differ. Otherwise prefer BOTH_EARS.

LEFT_VISOR.description: Left visor edge board, 53 LEDs chained behind LEFT_EAR on pin 19. The 5×8 grid is the main rendering region; beat-flash trio and connector LEDs have default behaviors.
LEFT_VISOR.when_to_use_directly: When creating asymmetric scenes. Otherwise prefer BOTH_VISORS.

RIGHT_EAR.description: Right ear diamond board, 16 LEDs. Use only for explicitly asymmetric content.

RIGHT_VISOR.description: Right visor edge board, 53 LEDs chained behind RIGHT_EAR on pin 21. Col-to-band mapping is reversed vs left so that bass frequencies show on inner columns of both visors.

MATRIX.description: Front-of-helmet 32×8 matrix. Thomas's 'main display' zone. Carries text, VU meters, sprites. Strict brightness ceiling of 45/255 enforced in firmware.
MATRIX.when_to_use_directly: Always — matrix has no mirror partner. All matrix content goes here.

BOTH_EARS.description: Virtual zone. A cue targeting BOTH_EARS renders identically on both ears. Preferred over separate LEFT_EAR + RIGHT_EAR cues for mirrored content.

BOTH_VISORS.description: Virtual zone. Renders to both visors with column reversal applied to the right side, so the same logical content appears on both.

LEFT_CONNECTORS.description: Override target for the connector LEDs on the left visor. By default, the trio runs a gold beat flash and the ring runs a slow rainbow cycle — without any cue targeting this zone.

RIGHT_CONNECTORS.description: Right-side connector LEDs. Same defaults as LEFT_CONNECTORS.

EFFECT_OFF.description: Turn all LEDs in the target zone to black. No parameters.
EFFECT_OFF.when_to_use: Use as a segment's base_effect when nothing should render outside of explicit cues. Use as the base_effect for an intro segment that begins in darkness. Use in a cue to explicitly silence a zone in the middle of a busy scene.
EFFECT_OFF.when_not_to_use: Avoid as the global base_effect for a whole scene — usually you want something subtle like a dim breathing palette instead.

params.name: color_index
params.description: Position 0–255 into the active palette gradient.

params.name: brightness
params.description: Scales the palette color. Subject to EAR_VISOR_BRIGHTNESS_MAX (185) in firmware.

EFFECT_EAR_SOLID.description: Fill the ear with a single solid color from the palette.
EFFECT_EAR_SOLID.when_to_use: As a static base layer during sustained passages. As a 'state indicator' showing which segment is active. For ear-LED moments where simplicity is right.
EFFECT_EAR_SOLID.when_not_to_use: Anywhere you want movement or beat-awareness. Pair with a more dynamic visor/matrix effect for contrast.

examples.context: A held magenta ear during a sustained chorus
examples.notes: Palette index 192 hits Stella pink in PAL_DISCOVERY. Brightness 160 is well under the 185 ceiling.

params.name: bottom_color
params.description: Color at BOT_PEAK (index 0). Renders through LOWER, FLAT, UPPER blending to top color.

params.name: top_color
params.description: Color at TOP_PEAK (index 15).

EFFECT_EAR_GRADIENT.description: Vertical gradient on the diamond. BOT_PEAK → LOWER → FLAT → UPPER → TOP_PEAK fades smoothly from bottom_color to top_color.
EFFECT_EAR_GRADIENT.when_to_use: Default ear-rendering effect for most segments. Sets up a held color palette that the matrix and visor can punctuate against.
EFFECT_EAR_GRADIENT.when_not_to_use: When beat-driven brightness modulation is wanted (use EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT instead). When a single solid color is enough (use EFFECT_EAR_SOLID).

examples.context: Warm bottom-to-bright-top gradient during a verse
examples.notes: Lit ember at the bottom of the diamond, brighter amber rising to TOP_PEAK. Sustained energy without motion.

params.name: decay_steepness
params.description: 0 = no decay (flat), 180 = tight beat-locked decay, 255 = very sharp. Match to music feel.

params.name: bottom_color
params.description: Static color at BOT_PEAK.

params.name: top_brightness
params.description: Peak brightness of the beat-cycled top color.

params.name: downbeat_flash
params.description: 0 = disabled. Otherwise peak white-flash brightness on TOP_PEAK on beat 1 of each bar.

params.name: subbeat_sparkle
params.description: 0 = disabled. Otherwise white sparkle brightness on BOT_PEAK at half-beat intervals.

EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT.description: The Crescendolls workhorse. Vertical gradient where the top color cycles through palette gradient indices in sync with the beat grid, with a brightness envelope that decays each beat. Optional downbeat flash on TOP_PEAK and sub-beat sparkle on BOT_PEAK add per-beat punctuation.
EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT.when_to_use: High-energy beat-driven sections — choruses, drops, dance-floor moments. Pairs beautifully with PAL_DISCOVERY where the beat-cycling shows off all four palette colors over each bar.
EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT.when_not_to_use: Quiet/atmospheric sections (use EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE). When the music has no clear 4-on-the-floor beat. When the beat grid in the scene header is unreliable.
EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT.notes: Reads the beat grid from the scene header. One full beat-color-cycle traversal takes 4 beats (one bar).

examples.notes: Deep indigo at the base (palette pos 0), beat-cycled bright colors at top, strong downbeat flash. The sparkle gives a hi-hat feel even between beats.

params.name: breath_period_100ms
params.description: Breath period in 100ms units. 40 = 4 seconds per breath. Typical 30–60.

params.name: min_brightness
params.description: Brightness at trough of breath.

params.name: max_brightness
params.description: Brightness at peak of breath.

EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE.description: Held vertical gradient that fades up and down on a slow sinusoidal cycle. The 'meditative' effect.
EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE.when_to_use: Intros, outros, atmospheric breakdowns, sustained passages where calm is wanted. Pairs with PAL_INTRO_WARM or PAL_OCEAN.
EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE.when_not_to_use: Beat-driven sections. Anything that needs urgency or rhythm.

examples.notes: 4-second breath, deep amber low to amber high. Restrained max brightness (130 of 185 ceiling) keeps the intro from competing with whatever the matrix is doing.

params.name: beats_per_pulse
params.description: 1 = one pulse per beat, 2 = per half-note, 4 = per measure

params.name: base_brightness
params.description: Dim base color for non-pulse LEDs.

EFFECT_EAR_EKG_PULSE.description: A bright pulse travels through the diamond: BOT_PEAK → LOWER → FLAT → UPPER → TOP_PEAK and back. Anchored to the beat grid.
EFFECT_EAR_EKG_PULSE.when_to_use: When the ear should feel 'alive' with rhythmic motion. Distinct from EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT — that fills the whole ear with each beat color, this is a discrete pulse traveling through.
EFFECT_EAR_EKG_PULSE.when_not_to_use: When the visor is already doing a lot of motion — the ear pulse might compete. Quiet sections.

EFFECT_VISOR_SOLID.description: Fill the 5×8 grid with one solid color from the palette. Connector LEDs run their defaults (or are addressed separately).
EFFECT_VISOR_SOLID.when_to_use: As a base layer during sustained moments. As a contrast to a more dynamic ear effect.

EFFECT_VISOR_GRADIENT_V.description: Vertical gradient on the 5×8 grid. Row 0 (bottom) = bottom_color, Row 7 (top) = top_color.
EFFECT_VISOR_GRADIENT_V.when_to_use: Setting up the visor as a held background. Pairs well with a beat-driven ear effect — keep visor static, ear dynamic.

params.name: inner_color
params.description: Color at col 1 (inner edge of visor).

params.name: outer_color
params.description: Color at col 5 (outer edge of visor).

EFFECT_VISOR_GRADIENT_H.description: Horizontal gradient. When mirrored to right visor, col mapping is reversed so 'inner edge = inner edge' semantic is preserved on both sides.
EFFECT_VISOR_GRADIENT_H.when_to_use: Cool moments where the helmet should have a directional color — e.g. cooler tones toward the center, warmer toward the edges.

params.name: vu_name
params.description: Name of a VU sequence in the scene's resources.vu_sequences array. Compiler resolves to ID. Must be a 5-band sequence.

params.name: single_color
params.description: Used only when bar_color_mode is single_color.

params.name: bg_color
params.description: Used only when bg_fill_mode is palette_color.

EFFECT_VISOR_VU_PRERENDERED.description: Render a pre-rendered 5-band VU meter on the visor grid. Each band corresponds to a column. Heights animate per the sequence's frame data.
EFFECT_VISOR_VU_PRERENDERED.when_to_use: When the visor should respond to the music's frequency content directly. Choruses, build-ups, sections where the energy spread across frequencies tells the story.
EFFECT_VISOR_VU_PRERENDERED.when_not_to_use: When VU data is not authored for this segment. When you want the visor to do something more spatial than just frequency bars.

EFFECT_VISOR_BEAT_WIPE.description: A color wave travels across the grid in sync with the beat. Picture: every beat (or every N beats), a column/row lights up bright then the wave moves on.
EFFECT_VISOR_BEAT_WIPE.when_to_use: Beat-driven sections where you want directional motion across the visor. Pairs well with a static ear effect.
EFFECT_VISOR_BEAT_WIPE.when_not_to_use: Mid-energy sections where motion would be too much. Outro/intro (use breathing instead).

params.name: rotation_period_beats
params.description: Beats per full rotation. 16 = 4 bars per rotation.

EFFECT_VISOR_HALO_ROTATE.description: A bright spot rotates around the perimeter of the 5×8 grid over a configurable period. Maintains a sense of motion at a slower-than-beat pace.
EFFECT_VISOR_HALO_ROTATE.when_to_use: Sustained groove sections where you want continuous slow motion without hard rhythmic emphasis. Crescendolls' Segment B (locked-in groove) used this pattern.
EFFECT_VISOR_HALO_ROTATE.when_not_to_use: Short segments — the rotation won't complete. Highly rhythmic moments (use BEAT_WIPE instead).

params.name: density
params.description: LEDs sparkled per second on average.

EFFECT_VISOR_SPARKLE.description: Random LEDs in the visor grid briefly light up and decay. Hash-based deterministic randomness — same scene time produces same sparkle pattern, reproducibly.
EFFECT_VISOR_SPARKLE.when_to_use: High-frequency hi-hat/cymbal moments. Building energy. Sections that need 'shimmer' on top of a held base.
EFFECT_VISOR_SPARKLE.when_not_to_use: When clarity matters — sparkle is distracting under text or sprites.

params.name: brightness
params.description: Hard-clamped to 45/255 in firmware regardless of authored value.

EFFECT_MATRIX_SOLID.description: Fill the entire matrix with one solid color.
EFFECT_MATRIX_SOLID.when_to_use: Almost never as the primary matrix content. Useful as a brief flash, a state indicator, or a fallback during sections you haven't designed yet.

params.name: vu_name
params.description: Name of a 32-band VU sequence in resources.

EFFECT_MATRIX_VU_PRERENDERED.description: 32-band pre-rendered VU meter on the matrix. Each matrix column is one frequency band.
EFFECT_MATRIX_VU_PRERENDERED.when_to_use: Choruses, drops, sections where the music's frequency spread is the main visual story. The matrix's VU is the most impressive thing it can do.
EFFECT_MATRIX_VU_PRERENDERED.when_not_to_use: Quiet sections (VU bars on near-silence look limp). Sections where you want the matrix doing text or sprites. Long full-song VU sequences are size-prohibitive (a 32-band 20 fps full song = 150 KB+); use VU only during segments where it really sings.

params.name: sprite_name
params.description: Name of a sprite in resources.sprites.

params.name: fps_override
params.description: 0 = use sprite's authored fps.

EFFECT_MATRIX_SPRITE.description: Play a sprite (small region animation) on the matrix at a fixed position. Sprites are stored in the scene's sprite library.
EFFECT_MATRIX_SPRITE.when_to_use: Signature visual moments — a spinning globe for 'Around the World,' a rotating record, a small animated icon during a recognizable hook. One-of-a-kind matrix content that VU and text can't capture.
EFFECT_MATRIX_SPRITE.when_not_to_use: Frequently — sprites are size-expensive (24 KB ceiling for all sprites in a scene combined). Use sparingly for impact.

params.name: text
params.description: Name of a string in resources.text_strings.

params.name: x_position
params.description: Negative starts text off-screen left, useful for scroll-in.

EFFECT_MATRIX_TEXT.description: Render text from the scene's text_strings array on the matrix. The glyph subset is auto-computed by the compiler from the strings array.
EFFECT_MATRIX_TEXT.when_to_use: Song-recognition moments — title flashes, key lyric callouts. HBFS's 'HARDER BETTER FASTER STRONGER' scrolling text is the canonical use case. Daft Punk songs often have minimal lyrics, so text is a high-impact element.
EFFECT_MATRIX_TEXT.when_not_to_use: Constantly — text everywhere becomes noise. Reserve for moments that genuinely benefit from words.

examples.context: Scroll 'HARDER' across the matrix during a vocal hit
examples.notes: Starts text off-screen-left (-24), scrolls right at slow speed, lasts 3 seconds — enough for the word to cross the matrix. Color 128 = helmet gold in PAL_DISCOVERY.

params.name: scroll_speed
params.description: Pixels per second.

params.name: pattern_period
params.description: Pattern period in pixels (e.g., 4 = stripes 4 LEDs wide).

EFFECT_MATRIX_SCROLL_BG.description: Scrolling background pattern. Useful as a base under text or sprites.
EFFECT_MATRIX_SCROLL_BG.when_to_use: When the matrix needs visual texture under foreground elements. Long-form sustained sections where 'something is happening' without big rhythmic moments.
EFFECT_MATRIX_SCROLL_BG.when_not_to_use: Alone — the patterns are too geometric to be the main visual. Always layer foreground elements (text, sprites) on top via additive flag.

params.name: peak_bright
params.description: Subject to per-zone caps in firmware.

params.name: decay_10ms
params.description: Decay duration in 10ms units. 20 = 200ms decay.

params.name: include_matrix
params.description: 0 = ears/visors only, 1 = include matrix (clamped to matrix ceiling).

EFFECT_FULL_FLASH.description: Bright flash across multiple zones at once. Punctuation effect for downbeat emphasis.
EFFECT_FULL_FLASH.when_to_use: Sparingly. Big downbeat moments, drops, climactic punctuation. The opening hit of a chorus.
EFFECT_FULL_FLASH.when_not_to_use: Repeatedly — flashes lose impact when overused. As a base effect. Sustained sections.

EFFECT_AMBIENT_BREATHE.description: Slow synchronized breathing across all ear and visor zones (and matrix if zone-targeted). Cross-zone variant of EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE.
EFFECT_AMBIENT_BREATHE.when_to_use: Outros, sustained calm moments where the whole helmet should pulse together. Pairs with PAL_OCEAN or PAL_INTRO_WARM.

EFFECT_CONN_SOLID.description: Override all 13 connector LEDs on one side with a single color, disabling the default beat-flash + rainbow behavior.
EFFECT_CONN_SOLID.when_to_use: When the scene wants the connectors silent or unified with the rest of the visor (e.g., 'everything goes red during the breakdown').
EFFECT_CONN_SOLID.when_not_to_use: When the defaults are doing what you want — defaults are usually fine.

EFFECT_CONN_BEAT_FLASH_OVERRIDE.description: Override the default gold beat flash on the connector trio with custom color/timing.
EFFECT_CONN_BEAT_FLASH_OVERRIDE.when_to_use: When the song calls for a different beat-flash color than gold (e.g., a red-themed segment where gold would clash).

params.name: cycle_speed
params.description: 0.1 Hz units. 5 = 0.5 Hz (full cycle every 2 sec).

params.name: hue_step
params.description: Hue increment per connector LED. ~25 default.

EFFECT_CONN_RAINBOW_OVERRIDE.description: Adjust the default rainbow ring's speed/saturation/brightness without disabling it.
EFFECT_CONN_RAINBOW_OVERRIDE.when_to_use: Quick tweaks to make the rainbow feel right for a specific scene's energy (faster for chorus, slower for breakdown, etc.).

complete_minimal_scene.description: A bare-minimum 30-second scene with one cue per zone. Useful as a template.
